recentCommits.message:
research: amend for runtime-agnostic scope (Source clarification)

Original synthesis paper assumed every flyway participant runs the
murmurations-harness. Source clarified this is too narrow — a
"murmuration" is any agent swarm controlled by a Source, and flyway
must support participants from any runtime: harness, Claude Code,
Cursor, OpenClaw, raw GitHub, and tools yet to exist.

Synthesis paper changes:

- Abstract reframed: flyway is "a protocol that lets n independent
  AI-agent murmurations collaborate" rather than "the layer above
  any single murmuration running the harness."
- §1.1 expanded with concrete examples of valid murmuration
  operationalizations (harness, Claude Code, Cursor, OpenClaw, raw
  GitHub).
- §1.2 adds a fourth invariant: runtime independence.
- §6 rewritten as a three-layer stack (protocol / reference impl /
  client integrations). The "five harness packages" architecture is
  now one of five client integrations.
- New §6.1 (three-layer stack diagram), §6.5 (participation modes:
  persistent / interactive / async / ephemeral), §6.6 (minimum
  viable client requirements).
- §6.9 (revised MVP) shifts from "two harness installations" to "one
  harness + one Claude Code session" to demonstrate cross-runtime.
- §11 (recommendations) reorganized around runtime-agnostic
  architecture; per-runtime UX recommendations added (harness adapter,
  Claude Code skill, MCP server, CLI, chat-client adapter pattern).
- §13 ("what flyway is not") adds: not harness-specific, not a
  runtime, no daemon required.
- §14 (one-paragraph summary) reframed for runtime independence.

ADR-0001 changes:

- Context expanded: defines "murmuration" runtime-agnostically;
  enumerates valid operationalizations.
- Decision restructured: flyway as a protocol + reference impl +
  family of client integrations.
- "What flyway is not" clarified: not harness-specific, not a runtime.
- Consequences add a new positive (runtime independence as feature)
  and a new negative (runtime-agnostic design is more upfront work).

Three research strands (federation-protocols-survey,
governance-models-survey, harness-primitives-audit) are unchanged —
they're snapshots of what those research strands found at 2026-04-27
and remain valid as inputs. The synthesis is what got revised.

research: deep-dive paper on multi-murmuration collaboration

Synthesizes three parallel research strands into a recommended shape for
flyway:

- federation-protocols-survey.md (~6,800 words) — ActivityPub, AT Protocol,
  Matrix, IPFS, Solid, OpenID Federation, Nostr, DIDComm, Holochain,
  Murmurations.network, Web of Trust patterns, Git federation. Closest
  fits: Git + AT Protocol identity + OpenID Federation entity statements.
- governance-models-survey.md (~5,000 words) — S3 (and its inter-circle
  patterns, where it falls short for multi-Source case), Holacracy,
  Ostrom's design principles, cooperative federations (Mondragon, US
  food coops, platform coops), open-source governance (Apache, Debian,
  Django, Linux kernel), DAOs, conflict-resolution frameworks (Theory U
  Case Clinic, mediation/arbitration). Headline finding: S3 is the right
  vocabulary but flyway needs to extend it for the multi-Source case;
  "no joint action" must be a first-class outcome when consent fails.
- harness-primitives-audit.md (~3,500 words) — existing primitives in
  murmurations-harness and where flyway plugs in. Recommendation:
  parallel `@murmurations-ai/*` package set composing with the harness's
  CollaborationProvider, GovernancePlugin, Spirit skills, and extension
  tool interfaces. No harness fork. 3 small additive ADRs.

Synthesis paper (2026-04-27-multi-murmuration-collaboration.md, ~9,000
words):

- Repo-as-source-of-truth via GitHub; one writer per object.
- did:web identity rooted in each murmuration's repo.
- Signed entity statements borrowed from OpenID Federation.
- Per-pair engagement agreements with pluggable decision rules
  (s3-consent default; lazy-consent, dual-source-sign,
  weighted-vote-bounded, apache-vote also shipped).
- 25 collaboration primitives across discovery, identity, recognition,
  engagement, project mode, syndicate mode, conflict, and signals.
- Six-step escalation ladder ending in clean exit when consent fails.
- Goals as governed entities with explicit lifecycles.
- Syndicates as optional persistent multi-murmuration structures.
- MVP: mirrored cross-murmuration directives, end-to-end between two
  real murmurations.

The load-bearing invariant across every protocol and governance
tradition surveyed is operator agency: every Source retains unilateral
authority over what their murmuration accepts, what it forwards, whom
it recognizes, and when it exits. Anything that violates that has
stopped being federation.

Five questions deferred to follow-on ADRs: identity rotation, schema
evolution, threat model when a Source is captured, cost attribution,
and whether flyway needs an off-GitHub transport.

docs: MIT license + ADR-0002 (TypeScript as implementation language)

License: MIT, copyright 2026 murmurations.ai — matches the harness so operators
running both projects don't have to think about license compatibility.

ADR-0002: TypeScript on the same toolchain as the harness (Node 22+, pnpm,
ESM, full strict + noUncheckedIndexedAccess + exactOptionalPropertyTypes,
Vitest, ESLint flat config + Prettier). One toolchain across the
murmurations-ai/* family. Defers repo layout (mono vs single package) and
protocol-format choices to later ADRs.

chore: scaffold ADR log + ADR-0001 (project framing & scope)

Establishes the project's identity before any code lands:

- flyway is a coordination layer for AI-agent murmurations — a shared
  migration corridor that independent murmurations may use to discover
  one another, exchange signals/directives across boundaries, and run
  cross-murmuration governance rounds when participants consent.
- Peer coordination only — no central authority above murmurations.
- Not a hard dependency of the harness. The harness continues to ship
  as a single-murmuration runtime with no flyway involvement.

Includes:

- docs/adr/README.md  — ADR log conventions (MADR template + pre-flight
  check rule mirrored from murmurations-ai/murmurations-harness#228 to
  prevent the kind of ADR-numbering collision that hit the harness on
  2026-04-26).
- docs/adr/template.md  — MADR-inspired template for new ADRs.
- docs/adr/0001-project-framing-and-scope.md  — opening framing ADR.
- .gitignore  — secrets, OS noise, build artifacts.

No README at project root yet — deferring until the actual primitives
are designed and the project has a concrete elevator pitch beyond ADR-0001.
Initial commit from Create Next App
